# Structure Routes, Intelligence Reasons

## Explains

Explains why the structural/governance layer and the intelligence/reasoning layer must be kept architecturally separate.

## The Concept

The governance system provides rails, routing, constraints, and structure. Intelligent entities (human or AI) provide judgment, reasoning, and decisions within those rails. Neither should do the other's job. This separation is architectural — not a preference, but a structural requirement for systems that need to be both stable and adaptive.

Governance that tries to reason becomes over-prescriptive. When rules attempt to encode judgment ("if the customer says X, respond with Y"), they become brittle — unable to handle cases the rule author did not anticipate. The rule is trying to be intelligent, and it fails because static structure cannot replicate dynamic reasoning. Conversely, intelligence without structure becomes chaotic. When reasoning entities operate without rails — no boundaries, no routing, no constraints — they produce inconsistent, uncoordinated, unreviewable outputs.

The separation creates a clean interface: structure says "you must go through this gate" and intelligence says "here is how I will satisfy the gate's requirements." Structure says "this decision requires these inputs" and intelligence says "here is my reasoning given those inputs." Neither layer is diminished — both are essential. But mixing them produces systems that are simultaneously too rigid (because the structure is trying to reason) and too chaotic (because the reasoning is trying to provide structure).

JOS IS the structural layer; ventures provide the intelligence. This separation is why JOS exists as a meta-OS rather than being embedded in each venture. The meta-OS provides routing, constraints, and governance architecture. Each venture — with its unique domain, people, and AI agents — provides the reasoning within those rails. The same structural layer supports many different reasoning entities, just as the same road network supports many different drivers.

## Why This Matters for Governance

This concept defines JOS's fundamental architecture. JOS does not tell ventures what to think or decide — it tells them what structure their decisions must flow through, what metadata they must capture, and what health criteria they must meet. The intelligence happens inside the ventures; the structure happens inside JOS. Violating this separation (JOS trying to make venture decisions, or ventures ignoring JOS structure) produces system failure.

This also guides how AI agents are governed. The governance layer routes work to agents, defines their constraints, and verifies their outputs. The agents reason within those constraints. Trying to make governance "smart" (encoding reasoning into rules) or making agents "self-governing" (removing structural constraints) both fail for the same architectural reason: they collapse two layers that must remain separate.

## Connects To

- JOS-C04 (Configuration Density) — configuration density determines how much structure is imposed on the intelligence layer
- JOS-C15 (The Agent Line) — the line between agent and system IS the line between intelligence and structure
- JOS-C20 (Composable Architecture) — composability requires clean separation between structural and reasoning components
- Framework §11 (framework/schema/architecture separation) — three levels of structural layer, all distinct from reasoning

## Provenance

Foundry "System Controls Routing, LLMs Control Reasoning" concept, reframed as a universal architecture principle. Reflects the model-view-controller separation in software, the distinction between process and judgment in organizational theory, and the constitutional separation between law (structure) and judgment (courts).

### Framework Connection

Framework §8 (governance vs. guidance dependency types) operationalizes the structure/intelligence separation by distinguishing structural dependencies (which route) from guidance dependencies (which inform reasoning), keeping the two layers architecturally clean.
